items.title: Harness Capability Compatibility Score
items.description: After every sync, display a per-harness compatibility percentage showing how much of your Claude Code config was faithfully translated vs. approximated vs. dropped. Users currently have no idea how much they're losing when they sync — a score like 'Gemini: 87% fidelity (3 skills unsupported, 1 MCP tool approximated)' makes the tradeoffs visible and actionable.

items.title: Cursor Rules & Agent Sync
items.description: Cursor is the most-used AI editor among professional devs. Adding a Cursor adapter that syncs CLAUDE.md rules into .cursorrules and translates skills into Cursor composer context would dramatically expand HarnessSync's reach. Pain point: developers who use Cursor daily and Claude Code for CLI work currently maintain two completely separate rule sets.

items.title: Aider Convention Sync
items.description: Aider uses .aider.conf.yml and convention files. Many developers use Aider for quick file edits while using Claude Code for complex tasks. Syncing coding conventions and project context would let them maintain consistent AI behavior across both tools. Solves the 'my AI forgets my conventions when I switch tools' problem.

items.title: Continue.dev Config Sync
items.description: Continue is the dominant open-source VS Code AI extension. Syncing context/rules into Continue's config.json and .continuerules would bridge the gap between IDE-integrated AI and CLI AI. Users who code in VS Code and occasionally drop into the terminal currently experience a jarring context switch.

items.title: Sync Profiles with Inheritance
items.description: Let users define named sync profiles (e.g., 'work-client-a', 'personal-oss', 'default') that inherit from a base and override specific fields. Currently syncing is all-or-nothing. A consultant working across multiple clients needs different MCP servers and rules per engagement without maintaining completely separate Claude Code setups.

items.title: Selective Component Sync
items.description: Allow users to sync only specific components — 'sync only skills to Gemini, sync everything to Codex'. Currently it's all-or-nothing. Users who have sensitive MCP server configs they don't want replicated everywhere, or harness-specific skills that shouldn't be translated, are blocked from using HarnessSync at all.

items.title: Git Branch-Aware Sync Profiles
items.description: Automatically activate different sync profiles when switching git branches (e.g., 'feature branches use strict no-file-deletion rules, main uses permissive rules'). Developers context-switch constantly between exploratory work and production work — their AI assistant should automatically adapt to the same context their code is in.

items.title: Team Config Snapshot Sharing
items.description: Export your full multi-harness config as a shareable bundle that teammates can import to instantly replicate your setup. Onboarding a new developer currently takes hours of manual harness configuration. A '/sync-export' command generating a portable bundle solves 'how do I get my team on the same AI configuration?'

items.title: Config Variable Templating
items.description: Support ${PROJECT_NAME}, ${GIT_USER}, ${REPO_URL} and custom variables in CLAUDE.md and skills, substituted differently per harness or project. Currently configs are static strings — developers who work on multiple projects copy-paste and manually edit configs, leading to drift and stale project references.

items.title: Drift Detection Alerts
items.description: Proactively notify users when a harness config has drifted from the Claude Code source (due to manual edits, harness updates changing format, etc.). Currently users discover drift only when something breaks or behaves inconsistently. A daily or on-demand 'drift check' with clear diff output prevents the 'why is Gemini ignoring my rules?' confusion.

items.title: Git Post-Checkout Sync Hook
items.description: Install a git post-checkout hook that triggers HarnessSync whenever you change branches or pull a team member's config changes. Removes the need to remember to run /sync after pulling — the most common reason configs drift is that developers forget to sync after updating CLAUDE.md.

items.title: Harness Update Impact Advisor
items.description: When a supported harness releases an update, HarnessSync detects it and flags which parts of your synced config may need re-translation due to format changes. Currently users discover breaking changes when their AI tool stops working. Proactive 'Gemini CLI v2.1 changed skill format — your 3 skills need re-sync' saves debugging time.

items.title: Unused Skill & Rule Detector
items.description: Analyze usage logs across harnesses to identify skills and rules that haven't been invoked in N days. Config bloat is real — developers accumulate dozens of skills over months, most of which go unused. Surfacing 'you have 12 skills that haven't been used in 30 days across any harness' enables conscious cleanup.

items.title: Natural Language Rule Builder
items.description: A '/sync-rule-add' command where users describe a rule in plain English and HarnessSync generates the appropriate format for each harness. Writing rules in CLAUDE.md syntax, then mentally translating 'does this work in Gemini?', is cognitively expensive. Natural language authoring with automatic multi-target output removes the format knowledge burden.

items.title: Cross-Harness Behavior Tester
items.description: Run a sample prompt against multiple configured harnesses and compare outputs to verify consistent behavior after syncing. The hardest part of maintaining multiple harnesses isn't the sync — it's knowing if the synced configs actually produce equivalent behavior. This makes regression testing across harnesses possible.

items.title: MCP Server Compatibility Matrix
items.description: Show which of your configured MCP servers are natively supported vs. approximated vs. unsupported per harness, with links to equivalent tools or workarounds. MCP is the highest-value part of the Claude Code ecosystem and the hardest to translate. Users need a clear map of 'what I'm losing' when syncing to each target.

items.title: Interactive First-Sync Onboarding Wizard
items.description: A guided '/sync-setup' flow that auto-detects installed harnesses, shows what will be synced, explains each translation step, and lets users opt-out of specific components before committing. New users face a blank-slate experience with no feedback on what will happen. A wizard reduces first-sync anxiety and abandonment.

items.title: Auto-Generated Sync Changelog
items.description: After each sync, generate a human-readable changelog summarizing what changed in each harness config ('Added 2 skills to Gemini, updated system prompt in Codex, removed deprecated MCP tool from OpenCode'). Developers running /sync blindly today have no record of what changed. A changelog makes syncs auditable and reversible.

items.title: Config Health Score & Recommendations
items.description: Score your CLAUDE.md and skills against best practices (clear instructions, harness-portable phrasing, no hardcoded paths, well-structured skill descriptions) and surface actionable recommendations. Users often don't know their configs are poorly structured until things go wrong across harnesses. Proactive scoring prevents cross-harness translation failures.

items.title: Multi-Project Config Overview Dashboard
items.description: A '/sync-projects' view showing all git repos where HarnessSync is active, their sync status, last-sync timestamp, and drift status in one place. Developers working on 5-10 projects lose track of which projects have synced configs. This provides a fleet-level view of config health.

items.title: Config Export as Human-Readable Docs
items.description: Export your full multi-harness config as a markdown document suitable for a team wiki or repository README — showing all active rules, skills, and MCP tools in plain English. Teams using AI tools together struggle to communicate 'how our AI is configured'. Auto-generated docs solve the 'what rules does our AI follow?' question.

items.title: Harness Migration Assistant
items.description: A guided workflow for users switching primary harnesses (e.g., 'I want to move from Codex to Gemini CLI as my primary'). Currently HarnessSync assumes Claude Code is the source of truth — but developers switching harnesses need help migrating their existing non-Claude-Code configs into Claude Code first.

items.title: Community Skill Library Browser
items.description: Browse and install community-contributed skills that are pre-verified to work across all supported harnesses. Developers spend hours writing skills that someone else has already perfected. A curated library of 'harness-portable' skills reduces duplication and ensures new skills start with good cross-harness compatibility.

items.title: Secret & Sensitive Config Filtering
items.description: Automatically detect and redact API keys, tokens, and sensitive env vars from synced configs, replacing them with safe placeholder references. Users who have API keys in their MCP configs today risk accidentally syncing secrets into less-secure harness config files. Automatic redaction makes sync safe by default.

items.title: CI/CD Sync Validation Action
items.description: A GitHub Action / pre-commit hook that validates all harness configs are in sync with Claude Code source before allowing a commit or PR merge. Teams who version-control their AI configs experience the same drift problems as code — this brings CI discipline to config management.

items.title: Translation Gap Suggestions
items.description: When a Claude Code feature can't be mapped to a target harness, suggest the closest native alternative and offer to configure it. Currently gaps are reported as 'not supported' — but users want 'here's how to get 80% of the behavior in Gemini CLI using native commands'. Actionable gaps are more useful than dead ends.

items.title: Visual Rollback Timeline
items.description: A '/sync-timeline' view showing all historical sync states with the ability to restore any harness to any previous snapshot. Currently rollback exists but is a single-step undo. Developers who need to revert 'the config from before last week's refactor' need time-travel, not just undo.

items.title: Auto-Discover Newly Installed Harnesses
items.description: Detect when a new supported AI harness is installed on the system (via PATH, homebrew, npm, etc.) and prompt to add it as a sync target. Currently users must manually run /sync-setup when adding a new tool. Auto-discovery closes the gap between 'I installed a new tool' and 'it has my config'.

items.title: PR Config Diff Comment Bot
items.description: When a PR modifies CLAUDE.md, skills, or MCP config, automatically post a comment showing the translated diff for each harness — 'this change will update Gemini's system prompt and add a new Codex tool'. Reviewers currently can't assess cross-harness impact of config PRs without manually running translations.

items.title: Skill Portability Linter
items.description: Lint skill files as you write them, flagging patterns that won't translate well to other harnesses (Claude-specific tool references, unsupported markdown extensions, etc.) with inline fix suggestions. Currently developers discover portability issues at sync time — catching them at authoring time saves debugging cycles.

items.title: Config Coverage Report
items.description: A report showing which aspects of your workflow are covered by config (file editing, testing, git, etc.) and which have no rules or skills defined. Developers often have config for obvious things but miss entire workflow areas. Gap identification helps build more comprehensive AI assistance.

items.title: Cross-Harness Usage Analytics
items.description: Track which harness you use most, which skills get invoked most frequently per harness, and surface insights like 'you use Gemini CLI 3x more than Codex — consider making it your sync priority'. Developers don't know which harnesses are worth maintaining. Data-driven prioritization focuses sync effort where it matters.

items.title: Config Size & Token Optimizer
items.description: Analyze your CLAUDE.md and identify sections that are bloated, redundant, or unlikely to fit in target harness context windows — with suggestions to trim or restructure. Long CLAUDE.md files get silently truncated by some harnesses. Proactive optimization ensures configs that work within every harness's limits.

items.title: Harness Config Sandbox
items.description: Spin up an isolated test environment that runs a harness with your synced config against a sample repo to verify behavior before applying to a real project. Currently validating sync correctness requires manual testing in your actual project. A sandbox provides safe, reproducible config verification.

items.title: Scheduled Sync with Email/Slack Digest
items.description: Configure HarnessSync to run on a schedule (daily, on git push) and optionally send a digest to Slack or email summarizing what changed across all harnesses. Teams who share AI configs need visibility into config changes made by teammates without checking manually. Push notifications close the feedback loop.

items.title: Context-Aware Rule Suggestions
items.description: Analyze your project's language, framework, and git history to suggest rules and skills you should add to Claude Code — 'You use pytest heavily but have no testing rules. Add one?' Developers with empty or sparse configs don't know where to start. Contextual suggestions bootstrap good configs from existing project signals.

items.title: Multi-Account Rule Federation
items.description: For users with multiple Claude Code accounts (work + personal), federate rules from both into a merged view per harness, with conflict resolution UI. Power users maintain separate Claude Code accounts for work and personal use but want a unified config in their other harnesses. Federation solves the 'I have to choose one' problem.

items.title: MCP Tool Stub Generation for Non-MCP Harnesses
items.description: When syncing to harnesses without MCP support, auto-generate shell script stubs that approximate MCP tool behavior (e.g., a bash script that wraps the same underlying API). Users who rely on MCP tools in Claude Code get at least degraded-but-functional equivalents elsewhere.

items.title: Interactive Conflict Resolver
items.description: When a target harness has local modifications that diverge from Claude Code's source of truth, surface a diff-based UI in the terminal letting users choose per-section: keep local, accept source, or merge. Currently drift is silently overwritten, which destroys intentional per-harness customizations users made directly in Codex or OpenCode.

items.title: Dry-Run Preview Mode
items.description: Add a `/sync --dry-run` flag that shows exactly what would change across all targets without writing anything. Displays a color-coded diff per target file. Users are afraid to run sync on production setups without knowing what will change — this removes that anxiety and builds trust in the tool.

items.title: Harness Health Dashboard
items.description: A `/sync-health` command that checks whether each target harness is installed, authenticated, and functional — not just whether config files exist. Shows version info, detected capabilities, and known compatibility issues. Users waste time debugging sync failures that are actually 'Gemini CLI not installed' situations.

items.title: Selective Sync Rules
items.description: Let users annotate CLAUDE.md sections with tags like `<!-- sync:exclude -->` or `<!-- sync:codex-only -->` to control what gets synced where. Some rules are Claude Code-specific (referencing MCP tools that don't exist in Codex) and blindly syncing them pollutes other harnesses with unusable instructions.

items.title: Reverse Sync Import
items.description: A `/sync-import` command that reads config from a target harness (Codex AGENTS.md, OpenCode skills) and merges useful content back into Claude Code. Users who started on Codex before discovering Claude Code have existing institutional knowledge trapped in AGENTS.md that they can't easily migrate.

items.title: Sync Profiles
items.description: Named sync profiles (e.g., 'work', 'oss', 'client-x') that control which accounts and targets are active per project. Switch with `/sync --profile work`. Users who consult for multiple clients need completely different sync targets per project and currently have to manually reconfigure every time.

items.title: MCP Capability Compatibility Matrix
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically detect which servers have native equivalents or partial support in each target harness, and surface a compatibility report. Users don't know which of their 20 MCP servers will actually work in Gemini CLI vs. silently fail — this removes the guesswork.

items.title: Auto-Sync on CLAUDE.md Save
items.description: Extend the existing PostToolUse hook to also trigger sync when Claude Code writes CLAUDE.md or settings.json via file-watching (fswatch/inotify). The current hook only fires on Claude Code tool use, missing the case where users edit config files directly in their editor.

items.title: Skill Coverage Gap Detector
items.description: Analyze which Claude Code skills have no equivalent in target harnesses and surface these as 'coverage gaps' with suggestions for how to approximate them. Users assume their Claude Code workflows are replicated everywhere but discover gaps only when they switch harnesses mid-task.

items.title: Sync Changelog Feed
items.description: Maintain a human-readable `.harness-sync/changelog.md` that logs every sync event with timestamps, what changed, and which targets were updated. Users in teams need an audit trail of when config changed and why — currently sync is a black box with no history.

items.title: Team Config Sharing via Git
items.description: A `/sync-export` command that generates a portable, shareable `.harness-sync/team-config.json` that teammates can import to replicate your multi-account setup. Onboarding new team members to the same harness configuration currently requires manual documentation and tribal knowledge.

items.title: Cross-Harness Prompt Benchmarker
items.description: Run a canned prompt against multiple harnesses simultaneously and show response quality, latency, and cost side-by-side. Helps users decide which harness to use for which task category. Users have no data-driven way to evaluate whether their Claude Code investment actually performs better than Gemini or Codex for specific workflows.

items.title: Permission Model Advisor
items.description: Detect when Claude Code's allow/deny tool permissions have no equivalent in a target harness and proactively warn with specific advice (e.g., 'Codex has no bash restriction equivalent — consider adding a guard comment in AGENTS.md'). Currently permission drift is invisible and creates security inconsistencies.

items.title: Harness Usage Analytics
items.description: Track locally (no telemetry) which harnesses are actually being used and how often, then surface this in `/sync-status` to help users decide which targets to keep syncing. Users often have 4 harnesses configured but only use 2 — the dead weight creates noise and slows sync.

items.title: Conditional Sync Triggers
items.description: Let users configure sync to trigger only under specific conditions: only on git branch changes, only when certain files change, or only at specific times. Power users want surgical control over when expensive sync operations fire rather than syncing on every Claude Code file write.

items.title: Skill Translation Engine
items.description: Intelligently translate Claude Code skill markdown into target-native formats, stripping Claude Code-specific tool calls and rewriting them as generic instructions. Currently skills are copied verbatim, leaving Codex with references to Bash/Edit/Write tools it doesn't understand.

items.title: New Harness Auto-Detector
items.description: Periodically scan PATH and common install locations for newly installed AI coding CLIs and prompt the user to add them as sync targets. Users install Windsurf or Cursor-CLI and forget to add it to HarnessSync — they only notice weeks later when it's hopelessly out of date.

items.title: Config Snapshot & Restore
items.description: Before every sync, snapshot the current state of all target configs so users can roll back with `/sync-restore --date 2026-03-10`. Users who push a bad CLAUDE.md change have no way to recover target harness configs that were overwritten.

items.title: Environment Variable Propagation
items.description: Sync relevant environment variables (API keys, tool paths, custom endpoints) across harness config files where the target format supports it, with warnings when a variable has no target equivalent. Users repeatedly re-enter the same API keys in every harness's settings.

items.title: Pre-Commit Sync Gate
items.description: A git pre-commit hook installer that runs sync-status before commits and fails if any target is out of sync, preventing commits with stale harness configs. Teams who review each other's CLAUDE.md changes need assurance that Codex and Gemini are always current.

items.title: Per-Harness Override Files
items.description: Support `.harness-sync/overrides/codex.md` files that get appended to the synced AGENTS.md for Codex-specific instructions. Users need to add harness-specific context without polluting CLAUDE.md — currently they're forced to either duplicate or go without.

items.title: AI-Powered Rule Optimizer
items.description: Use Claude to analyze CLAUDE.md and suggest which rules are redundant, conflicting, or too verbose for target harnesses, then offer to auto-optimize. Rules accumulate over time and many become outdated or contradictory — users have no systematic way to audit them.

items.title: Sync Notification Webhooks
items.description: After sync, POST a summary payload to a configurable webhook URL (Slack, Discord, custom endpoint). Teams using shared Claude Code configs in a repo need visibility when someone updates the rules that affect everyone's harness.

items.title: Feature Parity Report
items.description: A `/sync-parity` command that produces a structured report of every Claude Code feature in use (MCP servers, skills, rules, agents) with a per-target compatibility score and specific gaps. Gives users a clear picture of what they're giving up when working in a non-Claude harness.

items.title: Project-Aware Sync Scope
items.description: Detect the current git repo and automatically scope sync to project-level configs only when inside a project, falling back to global when at home. Users constantly forget to specify scope and accidentally sync global rules when they meant project-only.

items.title: Pre-Sync Config Linter
items.description: Before syncing, validate that CLAUDE.md and settings.json are well-formed, check for common mistakes (broken skill references, invalid JSON, missing required fields), and refuse to sync invalid configs with actionable error messages. Bad configs silently propagate to all targets and are hard to debug.

items.title: Terminal Status Bar Badge
items.description: A lightweight daemon that displays sync drift status in shell prompt or tmux status bar (e.g., `[HS: 2 stale]`). Users only discover drift when things break — ambient visibility means they address it proactively.

items.title: Bulk Account Onboarding Wizard
items.description: An interactive wizard that discovers all installed harnesses, detects existing account configurations, and sets up multi-account sync in one guided session instead of per-harness manual configuration. The current setup process requires separate steps per harness and account, discouraging full adoption.

items.title: MCP Server Registry Lookup
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically check a community registry for target-native alternatives (e.g., 'The GitHub MCP you use has a Gemini-native extension — want to use that instead?'). Users get better harness-native behavior rather than generic wrapped servers.

items.title: Scheduled Sync with Cron Integration
items.description: Allow users to schedule automatic syncs at off-peak times using system cron or HarnessSync's built-in scheduler. Users who make many rapid config changes don't want sync to fire on every change but do want eventual consistency without manual intervention.

items.title: Cross-Harness Secret Sanitizer
items.description: Scan source configs for patterns matching secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords) before syncing and either redact them or warn the user. Users accidentally commit API keys into CLAUDE.md which then get propagated to AGENTS.md that may be checked into version control.

items.title: Harness Adoption Coach
items.description: After sync, surface a tip about a Claude Code feature the user has configured that they haven't enabled in other harnesses yet, with a one-click setup. Users don't know what they're missing across harnesses — proactive coaching drives deeper adoption.

items.title: Config Evolution Graph
items.description: Track how CLAUDE.md and target configs have evolved over time and visualize the history in a terminal-friendly graph with `/sync-history`. Users working on long-lived projects want to understand when and why their config grew, and which rules have been stable vs. volatile.

items.title: Sync Self-Test Suite
items.description: A `/sync-test` command that creates isolated temp directories, runs a full sync cycle with fixture configs, and verifies correctness without touching real harness configs. Power users and contributors need a safe way to validate sync behavior after changes.

items.title: Community Rules Hub
items.description: A curated directory of shareable CLAUDE.md rule packs (e.g., 'TypeScript Best Practices', 'Python TDD Pack') that users can pull directly into their config with `/sync-install typescript-rules`. Users spend hours writing rules from scratch that the community has already solved.

items.title: Consider consolidating sync-* commands (5 commands)
items.description: There are 5 commands with prefix "sync-": sync-health.md, sync-parity.md, sync-restore.md, sync-setup.md, sync-status.md. Consider whether some could be subcommands of a single parent command for a cleaner UX.

items.title: MCP Tool Compatibility Matrix
items.description: Show which MCP tools work in which harnesses and flag tools that will silently fail. Users waste hours debugging why an MCP capability works in Claude Code but not in Gemini.
